title: Conjecture 1.3 (Alon–Hefetz–Krivelevich–Tyomkyn)
statement_text: Suppose $k \to \infty$ and $n/k \to \infty$, and consider $\ell$ satisfying $\binom{k}{2} - \ell = \Omega(k^2)$ and $\ell = \Omega(k^2)$. Then $\Pr(X_{G,k} = \ell) = O\!\left(1/\sqrt{k}\right)$.
context_text: Alon, Hefetz, Krivelevich and Tyomkyn initiated the study of anti-concentration of $X_{G,k}$ (edges induced by a uniformly random $k$-subset of an $n$-vertex graph $G$), motivated by connections to graph inducibility. Prior to this paper, Kwan, Sudakov and Tran proved the weaker bound $(\log k)^{O(1)}/\sqrt{k}$ using the Meka–Nguyen–Vu polynomial anti-concentration inequality.
paper_contribution: Proves a stronger Bernoulli-model version for $r$-uniform hypergraphs (Proposition 1.6): if $\ell = \Omega(k^r)$ then $\Pr(|X^{\mathrm{Ber}}_{G,k} - \ell| \leq k^{r-1}) = O(1/\sqrt{k})$, removing the polylogarithmic factor via Theorem 1.2.
notes: PDF source — the hypothesis on $\ell$ is garbled in the extraction; statement reconstructed from context and the discussion of Proposition 1.6.

title: Conjecture 1.5 (Alon–Hefetz–Krivelevich–Tyomkyn)
statement_text: Suppose $k \to \infty$ and $n$ grows sufficiently rapidly in terms of $k$. Then for all $0 < \ell < \binom{k}{2}$ we have $\Pr(X_{G,k} = \ell) \leq 1/e + o(1)$.
context_text: This is the central conjecture of Alon, Hefetz, Krivelevich and Tyomkyn on edge-statistics of random induced subgraphs, and the primary motivation for this paper's development of Poisson-type anti-concentration inequalities. The conjecture was proved independently by Fox–Sauermann [19] and Martinsson–Mousset–Noever–Trujić [29] before this paper's completion.
paper_contribution: Proves a Bernoulli-model analogue for $r$-uniform hypergraphs (Proposition 1.7): if $n/k \to \infty$ and $\ell \neq 0$, then for any $r$-uniform hypergraph $G$, $\Pr(X^{\mathrm{Ber}}_{G,k} = \ell) \leq 1/e + o(1)$, as a consequence of the new Theorem 1.10.

title: Conjecture 1.12
statement_text: Fix $p \in (0,1)$ and fix a graph $H$ with $h$ non-isolated vertices. Let $G \in G(n,p)$. Then for any $x \in \mathbb{N}$, $$\Pr(X_H = x) = O\!\left(1/\sqrt{\mathrm{Var}(X_H)}\right) = O\!\left(1/n^{h-1}\right).$$
context_text: The authors establish via Corollary 1.11 that $\Pr(X_H = x) = O(1/\sqrt{r})$ where $r$ is the number of edge-disjoint copies of $H$ in $G$, giving $O(1/n)$ in $G(n,p)$, but believe this is far from optimal. The conjectured bound matches the Gaussian scale and is best-possible given the known CLT for $X_H$. Theorem 1.14 confirms the conjecture for cliques $H = K_h$.
